body.purpose: Default text size floor across the whole app — 18px, never smaller for anything a user must read to act.

h1.purpose: One per screen, first thing read.

label.purpose: Always visible above every input; never placeholder-only.

shadows.name: floating-action
shadows.purpose: The persistent help FAB — signals it floats above the page.

shadows.name: ambient-card-lift
shadows.purpose: Student ID/estamara printable card — reads as a physical object, not a UI panel.

components.name: Primary Button (speed surface)
components.description: Full-width red pill button, the single primary action on scanning/attendance screens.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { width: 100%; font-size: 19px; font-weight: 700; padding: 16px; background: #D42027; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; font-family: 'Cairo', sans-serif; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #B91C1C; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #1A2744; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button (reject/negative)
components.description: White/red-outline counterpart to the primary button — never a second solid red.
components.css: .ds-btn-reject { width: auto; padding: 10px 18px; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; background: #fff; color: #D42027; border: 2px solid #D42027; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; font-family: 'Cairo', sans-serif; }

components.name: Nav Pill
components.description: Always-visible navigation link, no dropdowns or hidden menus.
components.css: .ds-nav-pill { display: inline-block; padding: 14px 20px; border-radius: 999px; background: #FFFFFF; border: 2px solid #E5E7EB; color: #1A2744; text-decoration: none; font-weight: 600; font-size: 16px; font-family: 'Cairo', sans-serif; transition: border-color 0.15s; } .ds-nav-pill:hover, .ds-nav-pill:focus-visible { border-color: #D42027; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Base content card — white surface on paper background, flat with a thin border, no shadow.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; border-radius: 14px; padding: 16px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; font-family: 'Cairo', sans-serif; } .ds-card strong { font-size: 19px; color: #1A2744; } .ds-card small { color: #5A6784; font-size: 14px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Full-width, large-target text field with an always-visible label above it.
components.html: <label class="ds-label">اسم الطالب</label><input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="اكتب الاسم هنا">
components.css: .ds-label { display: block; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 8px; font-size: 16px; color: #1A2744; font-family: 'Cairo', sans-serif; } .ds-input { width: 100%; font-size: 18px; padding: 14px 16px; border: 2px solid #E5E7EB; border-radius: 10px; font-family: 'Cairo', sans-serif; color: #1A2744; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #D42027; }

components.name: Teacher Pick Card (chip)
components.description: Whole-card tap target for picking a teacher — large unambiguous selected state, not a small checkbox.
components.css: .ds-pick-card { position: relative; cursor: pointer; background: #FFF5F5; border: 2px solid #D42027; border-radius: 14px; padding: 14px 16px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #D42027 inset; font-family: 'Cairo', sans-serif; } .ds-pick-card input { position: absolute; inset: 0; margin: 0; opacity: 0; cursor: pointer; } .ds-t-photo { width: 52px; height: 52px; border-radius: 999px; background: #F3F4F6; flex: none; } .ds-t-name { font-size: 17px; font-weight: 700; color: #1A2744; } .ds-t-schedule { font-size: 17px; font-weight: 800; color: #D42027; margin-top: 4px; }

components.name: Help FAB
components.description: Persistent fixed-position circular help button, present on every authenticated page, always in the same spot.
components.css: .ds-help-fab { position: fixed; bottom: 20px; inset-inline-end: 20px; z-index: 50; width: 52px; height: 52px; border-radius: 999px; background: #D42027; color: #fff; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 24px; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,.25); }

narrative.overview: Flat white cards laid out on a warm paper-white background, one big red action button per screen, oversized pill navigation you can hit without aiming carefully. The metaphor is a physical intake counter at a real clinic or government office: everything is out in the open, nothing is hidden behind a menu, and there is always exactly one thing in front of you to sign, tap, or hand over. This is the back-office counterpart to Harv's outward marketing brand — same red/navy/Cairo identity, but stripped of persuasion. Nobody here needs to be sold on anything; they need to finish the task in front of them and get back to the person waiting. The system explicitly rejects SaaS-dashboard visual language, landing-page persuasion, and cleverness that has to be learned. Two speeds, by task, not by screen family: speed-critical surfaces (scanning, attendance, the counter kiosk) get the loudest, biggest, most unmissable version of every component; deliberate surfaces (scheduling, teacher editing, the ledger) use a calmer, tactile-and-confident register — same tokens, less shouting.
keyCharacteristics: Flat surfaces, soft shadows only where physically necessary
One saturated color (red) reserved for the single primary action per screen
Pill shape as the universal tappable signal
RTL Arabic-first, plain-sentence labels, never icon-only
Big-by-default form controls — no compact/dense mode anywhere

rules.name: The One Red Rule
rules.body: Exactly one primary red button per screen. A second action gets a white/outlined-red secondary treatment, never a second solid red.

rules.name: No Decorative Gray
rules.body: Gray text is structural (borders, secondary metadata) or it doesn't exist. Never gray for anything the user must read to act.

rules.name: The No-Placeholder-Only-Label Rule
rules.body: A field's purpose is never conveyed by placeholder text alone — every input has a real, always-visible label.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow appears only when an element must feel physically separate from the page (floating, printed) — never as generic card decoration.

rules.name: The Two-Speed Rule
rules.body: Speed-critical surfaces (scanning, attendance, counter) get the loudest/biggest component treatment; deliberate surfaces (scheduling, teacher/staff management, ledger) use a calmer tactile-and-confident register. Same colors and shapes throughout — only visual weight and size shift. Never the reverse.

dos: Do keep exactly one solid-red primary action per screen
Do use the full pill shape for anything tappable
Do set body text at 18px minimum and labels always-visible
Do make speed-critical surfaces louder/bigger than deliberate surfaces, per The Two-Speed Rule
Do keep every navigation destination visible at all times; role-gate by omission, not disabling
donts: Don't build a SaaS-style dashboard: no gradient stat tiles, no chart-heavy insights framing, no hero-metric template
Don't use landing-page/marketing patterns: no hero sections, no persuasive copy, no promotional tone
Don't ship icon-only actions, swipe gestures, or hidden menus
Don't use a dense data-grid or keyboard-shortcut-first pattern anywhere
Don't use gray text for anything the user must read to complete a task
Don't add a second solid-red element to a screen that already has a primary action
Don't add decorative shadows to ordinary cards
